channel.title: Michael Mitchell on Bluesky
channel.description: Born Again Christian who loves technology. Wondering when I will get every electronic device I want.

item.title: On Public Service Day, 🧑‍💼 people share stories of kindness ...
item.description: On Public Service Day, 🧑‍💼 people share stories of kindness and dedication that remind us service isn’t just a job, it’s a way of life. ❤️ #PublicServiceDay #PublicService #ServeWithPride

item.title: After all these years without needing hard drives, I finally...
item.description: After all these years without needing hard drives, I finally need some, and they cost a fortune 💸. I want them for backups and other stuff 💾, but the prices are just ridiculous 😩. How is anyone supposed to build a high-capacity NAS ? #SelfHosting

item.title: The eagle’s sharp 👀 eyes remind us to look deeply at what fr...
item.description: The eagle’s sharp 👀 eyes remind us to look deeply at what freedom means in our own lives. 🦅 #AmericanEagleDay #BirdConservation #WildlifeObservance

item.description: TGIF! What a day it’s been, with just 5 hours to go. But who’s counting? 🤣

item.title: This was hilarious 😂, so I figured I’d share it.
item.description:

item.title:
USF Turns 70
As you get older, anniversaries and milestones...
item.description:
USF Turns 70

As you get older, anniversaries and milestones hit you differently. I don't know why. I don't regret anything in my life, but I do feel nostalgic sometimes. The other day, driving to work, I learned that the...

item.title: The meme is now legendary and even has its own emoji, “⁶🤷⁷.”...
item.description: The meme is now legendary and even has its own emoji, “⁶🤷⁷.” Learning something new on a Friday! #MemeCulture #FridayVibes #EmojiFun

item.title: The puckered texture of seersucker actually keeps the fabric...
item.description: The puckered texture of seersucker actually keeps the fabric away from your skin, making it perfect for warm days and fresh ideas. 👔🌞👖 #NationalSeeSuckerDay

item.description: Celebrating Daniel Landon's trip around the sun! Happy Birthday 🎉 my nephew! ☀️ #birthday

item.title: Well, got a lot done today. My home network is all running a...
item.description: Well, got a lot done today. My home network is all running at 2.5Gbps now, thanks to Unifi. The ISP line is still 1Gbps up and down. I might upgrade it, but not for a while. I also knocked a bunch of other things off my backlog ✅. Productive Saturday! #HomeNetworking #Unifi #TechUpgrade

item.title: Comet TV is airing Galaxy Guest live right now and the cast ...
item.description: Comet TV is airing Galaxy Guest live right now and the cast is fantastic. It’s such a funny show 😂, just what I needed on a Friday. I’m laughing so hard at this silly yet really good entertainment 🤣. I’ve seen this show plenty of times, but it’s been a while. #Entertainment #GalaxyGuest

item.title: What a weekend! 🚀 I set up a Gemini capsule site 🪐, got my o...
item.description:
What a weekend! 🚀 I set up a Gemini capsule site 🪐, got my own PeerTube instance running, and added https://michaelreflects.com to my Zola site 🌐. Everything’s now https://standard.site ready with Bridgy Fed, Bluesky, and the AT protocol. #SelfHosting #PeerTube #GeminiProtocol

Michael’s Journal
A quiet place for thoughts, tech, and reflections.

Samsung Gallery is ditching OneDrive integration

item.title: A lesser-known fact: Memorial Day 🇺🇸 was originally called D...
item.description: A lesser-known fact: Memorial Day 🇺🇸 was originally called Decoration Day because of the 🕯️ floral tributes left on 🎖️ graves. #MemorialDay #RememberAndHonor #Holiday

item.title:
AI Isn't Black and White
Okay, this will probably sound con...
item.description:
AI Isn't Black and White

Okay, this will probably sound controversial, but that's not my intent. I'm just sharing my thoughts because AI is everywhere right now, especially on social media and in blog posts. These...

item.title: Soldier dies after falling from horse at Royal Windsor Horse...
item.description:
Soldier dies after falling from horse at Royal Windsor Horse show

Soldier dies after falling from horse at Royal Windsor Horse show
Member of King’s Troop Royal Horse Artillery received medical treatment but died at scene after sustaining serious injuries A service person has died after falling from their horse after a display at the Royal Windsor Horse show, police said. The soldier, part of the King’s Troop Royal Horse Artillery, fell at about 7pm on Friday after exiting the arena. Continue reading...

item.title: As you all dive into your first thrilling adventures in Subn...
item.description:
As you all dive into your first thrilling adventures in Subnautica 2 Early Access, we wanted to share a little bit about our plans for the game over the next few months ✍️

Read more: https://unknownworlds.com/en/news/subnautica-2-early-access-roadmap

item.title: What a morning 😓. My Hetzner server crashed due to full stor...
item.description: What a morning 😓. My Hetzner server crashed due to full storage 💾. I tracked down the issue and fixed a bunch of things before work, and I’m already wiped out 😩. The biggest downside to running your own stuff is definitely all the maintenance and repairs. #TechLife #SelfHosting #Homelab

item.title:
Parsley, Pot Roast, and Changing Tastes
I saw this Bluesky ...
item.description:
Parsley, Pot Roast, and Changing Tastes

I saw this Bluesky post come across my timeline because I follow Food Republic, and it got my...

https://www.foodrepublic.com/2130936/1980s-meals-garnished-parsley/

https://mitchelltribe.com/2026/05/09/parsley-pot-roast-and-changing/

Why 1980s Meals Were Always Garnished With Parsley - Food Republic
From steak dinners to bowls of soup, 1980s restaurants topped nearly every dish with a sprig of parsley. But why was this garnish so ubiquitous?

item.title:
Parsley, Pot Roast, and Changing Tastes
I saw this Bluesky ...
item.description:
Parsley, Pot Roast, and Changing Tastes

I saw this Bluesky post come across my timeline because I follow Food Republic, and it got my attention. When I was a kid and teenager, I did not like parsley. But...

https://michaelmusings.com/post/parsley-pot-roast-and-changing-tastes

Parsley, Pot Roast, and Changing Tastes
<!-- Social card embed — saved from bluesky on 2026-03-30 --> Food Republic@foodrepublic.bsky.social · Mar 30, 2026🦋 BLUESKY From steak dinners to bowls of soup, 1980s restaurants topped nearly every dish with a sprig of parsley. But why was this garnish so ubiquitous? Why 1980s Meals Were Always Garnished With Parsley - Food Republicwww.foodrepublic.com View original post → “Why 1980s Meals Were Always Garnished With Parsley - Food Republic From steak dinners to bowls of soup, 1980s restaurants topped nearly every dish with a sprig of parsley. But why was this garnish so ubiquitous? — Food Republic (@foodrepublic.bsky.social) on bluesky (source)” I saw this Bluesky post come across my timeline because I follow Food Republic, and it got my attention. When I was a kid and teenager, I did not like parsley. But now, in my 50s, I actually do not mind it. I started using it after following some recipes from Chef Jean-Pierre, who has a YouTube channel. It really did make my pot roas

item.title: I am at the eye doctors for my pre cataract removal appointm...
item.description: I am at the eye doctors for my pre cataract removal appointment the only thing for sure i am getting a toric lens. Can't wait to see clearly again.

item.title: On Star Wars Day, we remember that sometimes the biggest jou...
item.description: On Star Wars Day, we remember that sometimes the biggest journeys start with the smallest steps—and a little ⚔️ faith. 🚀 #Maythe4th #StarWars
